title: Citrusy Juicy Moroccan Chicken Tagine
description: A romantic Moroccan dish featuring chicken cooked in a rich and flavorful citrusy sauce, perfect for a special occasion. This recipe uses extra virgin olive oil and is low-sodium-friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the chicken and cook, stirring occasionally, until it is browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in the dum, orange juice, lemon juice, and chicken broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 25-30 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through.
Stir in the chopped apricots and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste.
Garnish with chopped parsley and serve over couscous or rice.
